channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: THE cuckolds of crypto — 84 replies — 336.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
THE cuckolds of crypto

Open thread • Replies: 84 • Updated: 2026-05-18 08:51 UTC

OP

.

Early replies (first 3)

62238332
Fuck you bitch bastard

62238351
>>62238330
Insert "link" next tim pls, thanks!

62238706
>>62238330
so many Chainlink partnerships!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62242160
>>62242159
LINK has outperformed BTC
cope

62242176
>>62242160
BTC has consistently outperformed LINK since 2018
cope

62242184
>>62242176
>since 2018
since 2021
blind nigga

62242191
simple as

62242193
>>62242184
He’s right you retarded nigger, if you bought Link in September 2017 you got outperformed by BTC. Congrats on being poor

62242204
>>62242193
What if you bought ETH at that same time?

Why doesnt this board have +10 ETH fud threads up all the time

62242205
>>62242193
he's a paid shill, don't even bother
he even fucked up and confused the fiat ATH with sats ATH
total imbecile

62242216
>>62242204
nobody is desperately trying yo convince me that ETH hasn't underperformed, get it now?

62242225
>>62242193
>if you bought Link in September 2017 you got outperformed by BTC
Wrong!
See:
>>62241119
LINK has outperformed BTC,
you can cope as much as you want fuddies
but it's just the facts

62242230
>>62242216
The narrative is that LINK has been worst investment you could have done back in 2017-2018 when ETH has performed 10x worse.

ETH doesnt even have Sergey dumping tokens so how is that possible?

62242249
>>62242225
LINK is sitting comfortably about -30% from its 2018 sats high, sorry but it's simple facts, it has underperformed BTC for almost 8 years now, each and every buy you did after that fact is in the red, and you clinging on to the ICO and Binance listing pump is disingenuous at best
sorry pal, can't argue with reality
>>62242230
you seem confused, that's YOUR cope narrative, that LINK was the only investment you could have made during 2017-18, while everyone else is simply pointing out to you that it's been a trash investment ever since
are you also retarded, like your gaslighting friend, or are you merely pretending?

62242250
>>62242193
>if you bought Link in September 2017 you got outperformed by BTC

LINK price (Sept 2017): $0.10
LINK price now: $9.69
LINK % gains: 9,590%

BTC price (Sept 2017): $4,000
BTC price now: $78,000
BTC % gains: 1,850%

LINK OUTPERFORMED BTC BY: 7,740%

62242252
>>62242249
No i just said that you could have invested in ETH for example

Why does that rile you up? Why arent you spamming catalog full of ai slopped Vitalik pics & ETH/BTC chart?

62242253
>>62242250
>the disingenuous shiller continues to play dumb and engage in shameless gaslighting
honey, the Sept 2017 ATH was $1.50, not $.010
the state of this board

62242258
>>62242252
cool, I'm just saying that LINK has been performing like garbage for almost 8 years, what the fuck is YOUR problem?

62242267
>>62242258
But LINK has literally outperformed BTC since 2018?

And pretty much the whole market

62242270
>>62242267
LINK is literally down from it's 2018 high in sats???
can you read a fucking chart bro?

62242275
>>62242253
>honey, the Sept 2017 ATH was $1.50
wtf are you smoking?

62242278
>>62242270
But it literally isnt? What kind of charts are you looking at

Anyway i literally couldnt care less, never selling

62242280
>>62242278
He's just making stuff up now it seems
lmao I broke the fuddie bot

62242285
keep embarrassing yourselves, that woll surely convince people to buy your heavy bags

62242297
>>62242285
No one here sold when it literally dumped to 0.00001 , what are you trying to achieve?

62242324
>>62242285
You don't need to buy Link. Actually I am totally happy currently with people not buying Link. I wish the price would crash even harder now that I am not a poorfag student anymore and have the money to buy more.
Chances are high I will still be very wealthy in a few years while you will stay a dumb useless shitskin anyway sorrounded by other shitskins, without any prospects in your pathetic life other than posting shit online for a few cents a day.

62242431
>>62242116
rattled plus whiter than you, moroccan transplant
still not posting portfolio huh? show us how you picked all the "winners" (lol) from 2017, go ahead

62242727
>>62238330
sirgay plz pump this shit already
i can't w8 any longer

62243376
>>62242431
His life is basically hell
He's a sandnigger who is obsessed with nigger dicks, and is such a gooner that it makes him suicidal
He also sold most of his link low (after buying at $30) to buy linksol and layerzero (and we know how those turned out LOL,) hence the obsessive rage over link and link holders

62244078
>>62243376
The lil sand nigger got btfo here so he's making like 10 new fudfag threads

62244381
>>62242431
>>62243376
Based Adem mind raping cuckold baggies.

He WON
Linkies LOST

62244443
I'm... KABOOOOOOMING

62244446
>>62244443
pic related is basically kryptonite for the cucks. They can never recover from this tweet

item.title: how do LINK shills refute this? — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
how do LINK shills refute this?

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-18 08:57 UTC

OP

how do LINK shills refute this?

Early replies (first 3)

62240980
>>62240977
Self checked

62240982
>>62240977
cringe CLL meme. price + chart + take the L

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62240992
>>62240967
If i invested in BNB that chink fuck would have rugged it and LINK would be $5000

62240993
>>62240982
That's a chart going up.

62241005
>>62240967
You are a fucking bastard

62241326
>>62240967
They’re called cuckolds for a reason

62241446
link has been fucked into the dirt, sadly

62241871
>>62240982
how does one "take the L" specifically?

62241990
>>62240992
this and everyone knows it. I've never seen a bnb portfolio posted on this board over 6 figures, hell even 5 figures for that matter.

62242019
>>62241990
because this board is trash, people are still shilling LINK after 9 years, baggies are also shilling GME. This board is a cesspool full of schizo baggies

62244013
>>62242019
This. Old /biz/ had mad alpha. The OG /biz/fags were shilling $0.50 ETH and $300 BTC, all the people from that era got stupid rich and quit the board.
What do we have left? A bunch of broke neet schizos and zoomers shilling their dead shitcoin bags.
I dont think I've even seen SanDisk or MU being shilled here besides in the /smg/ general, that and /pmg/ are the only 2 productive threads on this entire shitheap board. /biz/ totally lost its edge after the 2021 bullrun and jeet invasion.
Reddit and x are mogging /biz/ nowadays. Normies are getting rich from tech stocks while /biz/cels are bagholding chainlink from a decade ago and they havent made any gains in years

62244025
>>62244013
link boom
so boom
bullish link
kaBOOOOOOM

62244248
>>62240967
>refute that the crypto market is fake and gay!

We know it is. Take one look at the top 10.
Why do you think crypto price action is up ass creek while stocks are at ATH?

62244357
>>62244013
>while /biz/cels are bagholding chainlink from a decade ago and they havent made any gains in years
speak for yourself you stupid nigger. I have 40k LINK staked since v0.1 and im up 600% ytd in my stock portfolio thanks to semis, memory and photonics.

you dumb pieces of shit are ALWAYS projecting your poorfag spirit onto others. i dont post alpha here cuz i refuse to give crumbs to LINK fudders kek

62244374
>>62244357
your shitcoin is down -85% since 2021. It was the most atrocious investment ever, nobody needs your shitty crumbs about LINK lmao

62244460
>>62244374
Good thing you devoted your entire life to thinking and posting about this all day every day.

item.title: OH NO NO NO NO — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
OH NO NO NO NO

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-18 08:50 UTC

OP

BAHAAHGHAHAHGAHAHA

Early replies (first 3)

62241118
>>62241023
Yeah when you mine BTC when its sub $10 and then buy BTC with ur VC company when its $30, you will be rich

62241137
>>62241023
Why do you care how rich someone is? Was Sergey your middle school bully or something?

62241143
>>62241118
it also helps to sell 400k - $1 mil usd in link on binance every week for 8 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62241144
>>62241137
no i bullied him and now he keeps sending me "monthly updates" of his net worth and pictures from his yacht. i keep changing emails but he somehow finds the new one every time

62241192
scam-artist

>and his shitlinker-cultist follower still dont get it!

62241228
>>62241143
He said this was exactly what he was going to do, 9 years ago.
Yet here you are, crying about it every single day.

You're not a real person.

62241472
>>62241137
Sergey is a disgusting fat scammer and I like mocking fat people

62241474
>>62241023
The Arch Goy strikes again

62244430
>>62241023
KABOOOOM

62244441
such BULLISH KABOOOOOOM

62244445
Sirgay betray

item.title: When did you realize that LINK has actually outperformed BTC? — 14 replies — 43.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
When did you realize that LINK has actually outperformed BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-18 08:41 UTC

OP

When did you realize that LINK has actually outperformed BTC?

Early replies (first 3)

62242213
I bought in 2022 and I am unironically financially ruined

62242243
>>62242199
classic PnD shitcoin chart

62242254
>>62242243
That's a chart of LINK outperforming BTC.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62242277
>>62242199
double bottom? if not it is over

62242288
>>62242254
you need to set better expectations for yourself. celebrating that terrible chart is sad.

you can do better.

62242294
>>62242288
LINK price (Sept 2017): $0.10
LINK price now: $9.69
LINK % gains: 9,590%

BTC price (Sept 2017): $4,000
BTC price now: $78,000
BTC % gains: 1,850%

62242300
>>62242213
Same, my average is $15 @ 3000 link.

62242990
>>62242294
ok BUT: cope

62243153
>>62242294
Hmm. This is interesting.

At ICO in September 2017:
1 LINK = 0.0001278 BTC

May 18, 2026:
1 LINK = 0.0001251 BTC

62243997
>>62242243
Kek. Anyone who didnt buy ONLY in 2018 has lost btc. Anyone that bought any link at all in the past 7 years has lost btc.

62244019
>>62243997
To be fair if you bought it at ICO in 2017 you still have roughly the same amount in satoshi value, it hasn't lost against BTC. It's just that if you sold at many times before now you would have more BTC. Most other older cryptos have lost a lot more relative value against BTC so if you've held since day one, it was a good store of value.
Eg. If you'd held litecoin or eth since 2017 you'd be down a lot more than holding link

So that's nice I suppose

62244339
>>62244019
The stress from holding it for 8 years definitely shortened a lot of lives. Just because it was great in theory didn’t mean that Sergey couldn’t fuck up somehow. It was a high risk play relative to BTC.

At least with BTC you knew that it would recover after every bear market. So holding LINK for that long definitely came at a steep price in terms of mental health

62244422
>>62244019
Link was up x50 against BTC but the cuckolds got greedy and here we are

62244425
Uh yeah

item.title: I've lost 40 years worth of investing and hard work so... — 18 replies — 31.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've lost 40 years worth of investing and hard work so...

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-18 08:26 UTC

OP

...so let me cope before I kill myself.
ITT we discuss povertism and how money is useless and not important.

Early replies (first 3)

62243479
Buddhist monks own nothing and are unironically happier than billionaires

62243636
>>62243479
There should be a way of reaching one's goals other than through money; also, not everyone with money reaches his goals.
Fuck my life!

62243648
>>62243452
Did u buy 1dte calls on Friday

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62243722
>>62243479
donations, communal support, temple assets, and very low personal expenses.

they are glorified beggars under the guise of cleanliness, discipline and seeking enlightentment. You could live with next to no money if you lived the way they do. They live by spending no monney becasue the holes are plugged up by the aforementioned donations and support. but in reality u do not want that life cuz.

>>62243636
Depends on the goals, but yeah i'd say 90% of goals require money. And the goals that don't require money require time and attention that most ppl cant afford to put into it cuz they dont have enough money lmao.

62243783
>>62243452
howd you do that OP
help us learn from your example

62243804
>>62243722
fuck off, why do you want to kill me?

62243807
>>62243722
They actually make most their money from teaching martial arts

62243840
I HATE THIS!!!

62243844
>>62243452
I keep warning /biz/ about this

most people lose everything not just once, but several times in life. It's normal. Learn to deal with massive disappointment. Don't let it ruin your day.

62243847
>>62243844
there must be a way to live without money!!!!

62243866
>>62243847
if you're in the US it's pretty easy to live without money. Not super comfy, but easy

62244008
^^^^^^^^^^AT LEAST I DON'T HAVE TO DEAL WITH INEQUALITY^^^^^^^^^^
HAHAHAHAHAHA

62244250
I really want to die!

62244257
>>62243844
:(

62244275
>>62243452
>povertism? No no
>pervertism - yes yes
Hang out in parks and creep on MILFs and tween girls.

62244277
>>62243452
Men were meant to build, women were meant to consume. I get no pleasure from buying things.

62244309
>>62244277
what do you mean?

62244383
>>62244275
:(

item.title: Shilling this Brazilian magic internet money because their CMO probably ended up in a trash bag in a dark alley. — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Shilling this Brazilian magic internet money because their CMO probably ended up in a trash bag in a dark alley.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-18 08:48 UTC

OP

Shilling this Brazilian magic internet money because their CMO probably ended up in a trash bag in a dark alley.

Currently at pennies and mainnet is going operational next week since it was delayed for LayerZero hardening

Early replies (first 3)

62243345
I'm holding 600k of this anime girl token. Aigmi?

62243865
>>62243345
Yes, once this pullback is over and that the mainnet is finally operational just this next week (LayerZero is almost done with contract calls in the blockchain explorer). YGMI.

Omnichain + new listings this week, they deployed a new liquidity pool for an upcoming CEX.

https://explorer.rayls.com/

62244000
Get your cheap Brazilian RWA tokens while you still can

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62244199
>>62242748
Why is Amelia advertising it?
It does make it appealing but try to be more racist.

62244221
>>62242748
>>62243865
>LayerZero
Dropped.

62244442
>>62242748
>I'm dumb enough to buy a brazillian scam.
>Please bail me out.
Get fucked chud.

item.title: People only want the money — 18 replies — 17.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
People only want the money

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-18 07:58 UTC

OP

This is why crypto is dying, people were hyped about it at first because you could MAKE MONEY with it

Everyone in the end just wants the money, nobody cares about the product, decentralization all of that jazz

Early replies (first 3)

62241498
where does the money come from

62241500
>>62241498
goverment?

62241505
WHERE'S THE MONEY
WHERE'S THE MONEY
WHERE'S THE MONEY
WHERE'S THE MONEY
>this is literally what 99% of population thinks about

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62241540
>>62241495
Initially (2010 - 2013) people actually believed Bitcoin would be revolutionary, then those early adopters involved in the space realized that it actually can't scale and decided to just grift baggies with alts (2014 - 2017). Then in the last shred of hot air legitimacy, crypto made a run on "defi" (2018 - 2020) but the baggies soon realized even defi is a scam for money that will never be adopted by the masses and stopped falling for alts, icos and white papers, instead accepting the scam for what it is and kicking off the degenerate era, shamelessly buying trash like meme coins, animal coins, NFTs and shit coins brazenly through AMMs, also dragging in and scamming the last wave of normies that will ever buy crypto (2020 - 2023). Which brings us to the current era, heat death, where all the normies know it was a scam all along and there's nothing special about it so they've moved on to gamble back in the traditional markets. The only people left are dinosaurs that buy BTC and forever baggies holding zombie projects like LINK and praying for a miracle.

62241545
>>62241540
best summary there is thanks anon

62241548
>>62241495
I've been researching the tech for 5 years. Many networks are very close to releasing user-friendly products. Up to this point, these networks have only been accessible to geeks.

62241564
>>62241548
nobody cares about user friendly. Where's the money? Does this make money for me? if yes then its a hit, if no then its a miss

62241684
>>62241495

If you believed in the product, you wouldn't be on the internet crying about what other people are doing.

62241687
>>62241684
smartass

62241689
>>62241687

dumbass.

>>62241500

62241704
>>62241564
You don't understand the tech. Servers are far more expensive than crypto integrated networks.

62241714
Now you finally understand the obvious. If you still delude yourselves into thinking that tech matters to people, then ask yourselves, "why did fucking memecoins, many of them at that, went well over a billion dollars in market cap?"

62241729
>>62241714
Demand matters, but demand rooted in utility is far more sustainable.

62241741
>>62241495
>decentralization all of that jazz
NSA literally invented it and have a back door programmed in for all the other spook agencies to do what the fuck they want to buyers of it.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8QdCvssobis

62241756
>>62241495
Enjoy tax-cattle slavery, me and my homies will be gettin' da self-sovereignty surviving what's coming with untraceable crypto-currencies

62241769
>>62241540
Pretty much yeah.

Literally zero reason to buy crapto. Virtually zero reason to buy BTC.

Everyone just gambles on options / predictions markets now.

62241774
>>62241704
>I'm in it for the tech!
He said the line. Kek.

62244346
>>62241714
>wheres the food at?!
>wheres the money ?!
>more MORE!!!!!!!!

item.title: turns out the paid shills were wrong about muh DTCC partnership — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
turns out the paid shills were wrong about muh DTCC partnership

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-05-18 09:00 UTC

OP

and it’s another nothingburger. Lol

Early replies (first 3)

62244464
>>62244450
>wrong about muh DTCC partnership
DTCC announced it themselves
https://x.com/The_DTCC/status/2054172654135873638

62244469
>>62244464
people invest in crypto to make money, nobody cares about partnerships if they have ZERO effects on the price

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: I PUSH MY FINGERS INTO MY EYES — 7 replies — 3.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
I PUSH MY FINGERS INTO MY EYES

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-18 07:02 UTC

OP

I PUSH MY FINGERS INTO MY EYES

Early replies (first 3)

62243875
>>62243863
HOLD ME NOW..

62244129
>>62243863
Unfortunately, the pain went on.

62244213
>>62243863
THE BEST

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62244218
Someone made a reggae version of that...slaps ON GOD no cap frfr

62244223
>>62244218
Shit...i mean bossa nova...reggae is reggay

62244224
IF THE PAIN GOES ON, IM NOT GONNA MAKE IT!!!!!!!!!!!!

62244260
And the rain will kill us all

item.title: why does the idea of FIRE make normies so angry? — 52 replies — 32.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does the idea of FIRE make normies so angry?

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-05-18 07:23 UTC

OP

why does the idea of FIRE make normies so angry?
>you're not living life because you have a coffeemaker and a cheap car!
something about it just instills rage in them, they can't stop talking about how much they hate working, yet it's a bad thing to stop working early?

Early replies (first 3)

62242785
>>62242760
Not giving you anymore viewcounts. buy an ad.
CLEAN IT UP JANNIES.

62242793
>>62242760
Yeah I'm totally not living my life by making my own coffee and not having a car payment

62242802
>>62242760
>whats the point of early retirement
To not work.
What the fuck kind of question is that
I don't want to fucking work. I am willing to live like a monk if it means getting to that goal early.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62243455
How do you guys date women and FIRE?

I have low 7 figs, enough to not wage and live a simple but comfortable life, but I've never been in a relationship and don't know if women would accept a non-materialistic lifestyle. What if she wants to buy the latest trendy sloppa. What if she freaks out at 40% market downturns. What if she feels FOMO seeing her friends instagram travel photos.

I can live like a prison inmate and ruthlessly cut out goycattle consoomerism but would women really tolerate dating a guy like that?

62243458
>>62243393
This hits the nail on the head. Combination of people going way too far with the penny pinching while also just being generally smug elitists.

62243465
>>62243455
>How do you guys date women and FIRE?
a lot of women earn decent money now. just let her use her money for dumb shit. keep it separate as much as you can.

62243473
>>62243455
Women are cheaper to rent than own

62243478
>>62243455
>how do you guys swim in a pool without getting wet?

62243492
>>62242828
No shit sherlock. "Working" to take care of yourself, your family, your beliefs is entirely different than spending 1/3rd of your life doing something for company that you don't care about and doesn't care about you.

62243501
>>62242881
>hen one takeaway I could observe is that they equate having the latest and greatest with happiness
Spot on. 9 out of 10 times they are filling a void with consumption. I get it, I do it sometimes too, we all do. But there's a difference when buying stuff is your only source of happiness.

62243513
>>62242760
Slave ship logo kek, reel world.

62243518
This thread keeps getting reposted

62243540
>>62243478
It also keeps those money stealing hoooores away from you.

62243776
I don't know what "FIRE" this is referencing, but I imagine they deployed the term to keep the "FIRE" sector term (Finance, Insurance, Real Estate) out of popular consciousness. I doubt they want people ruminating on how fake the economy is.

62243861
>>62243192
>>62243350
>having a wife
shiggy diggy

62243873
>>62242760
It doesn't matter if you invest big in the system or invest small in the system if the system is doomed

62243944
>>62242760
wagies who shit on the corolla spend 15 hours a week sitting in a cuckover on their way to serve mr goldberg.

62243947
>>62243281
A BMW and a Ford Fiesta are not the same.
Some people just like nice things.
But for me the death blow to FIRE is their anti-natalism.
I have 4 kids and I'll gladly work an extra 20 years if that's the price.
I also like buying my kids toys and so on that make them happy.

62243961
>>62242760
It's a common misconception that you need to live like a monk in order to FIRE. Not true at all. I invested most of my disposable income when I was working but I still occasionally splurged on going out with my friends to concerts and music festivals, international travel, and a nice car. The key is moderation and consistency.

62243965
>>62243947
this is actually one of the secrets to a good retirement

living frugal only prepares you to lose money

if you raise kids, every time one hits 18 or gets a job your costs go down. Handicap principle. Like running a marathon with 100lbs on your back but half way through the weight comes off and you pass everyone.

62243971
>>62243947
>But for me the death blow to FIRE is their anti-natalism.

u got me there, my favorite thing is peace & quiet.

yeah being married with children is financial suicide. in this society where u are judged by what u spend money on, treading water forever, fuck that shit

62243979
>>62243971
I paid cash for our family house and my wife is a medical doctor about to finish residency so I still plan to retire a millionaire even with the 4 kids (and we might have a 5th).

62243985
>>62243979
>>62243979
that's nice, I am happy for u, just saying i can't do it anyways, i need to be a hermit anyways

62244052
>>62243393
my gripe is some of the fire fags I've talked to have some of the most obnoxiously hedonistic NPC mindsets ever. Yeah just make as much money as possible doing whatever job pays the most. Not because you're passionate about something or enjoy it. Just comes off as extreme procrastination teetering into a mental illness
>yeah when I retire at 40 then I'll do what I want to!
>I'll go travel and see the world!

Like you can literally do all that shit in your late 20s and 30s if you have a good job and still retire early. These people will be insane by their 60s

62244092
I'm buying $1200 a month in index funds, about 16% of my income. I could FIRE but I would have to work more AND sacrifice on lifestyle and fuck that.

62244097
Public school exists primarily to produce perfectly docile worker bees.

You opting out of the system by retiring early is a betrayal to their entire world view.

62244118
>>62244097
Public school was a Prussian invention, the country that pioneered your chud ideology. Wasn’t it supposed to be the most based country to have ever existed?

62244196
>>62243455
Women want resources because they want to start a family. As much as the kikes wanted to destroy our civilisation with feminism and attacking family values, they still have this primal instinct to measure men by their ability to provide resources and often safety. They just suck at telling especially the latter.
Sadly few women will go for broke arse men.
Meanwhile men just want a pretty woman who's not fucked in the head, and they suck at telling the latter. But since they are more desperate, they will settle for good enough. The con is men tend to bring a lot to the table and women do not (but they may pitch much more of their time than you do when you have kids). The pro is many men end up having a good mid-life, while women who ran through a cock carrousel and wanted Chad get a lonely, bitter one - seen this in my friends while I'm happily married to a trad wife (and yes, I provide a very good life while she devotes herself to our kids).

62244207
>>62242760
because you can't have a wife and kids whilst engaging in this, it just doesnt really work.
And if you're not working towards a wife and family, you're just kinda wasting your life.

62244209
>>62242760
Well it usually foregoes having children, although most men who FIRE with a woman involved end up with 1 kids due to female biological instincts.
I know a couple from Germany who stole about $2 million in COVID funds and fled to Paraguay and they ended up with a baby even tho they were both saying all thru their 20s they'd never have kids.

62244228
>>62243985
how old are you anon? loads of people have the most fucktarded opinions and beliefs in their 20s, mostly due to retarded upbringings, how old are you?

62244273
>not consooming a new car is a fried brain
Lol mutts are funny

62244292
>>62244273
4chan is just contrarian

most of the US is driving a new car if they can.

while most of 4chan is american, most americans are nothing like 4chan

item.title: WHAT IS HAPPENING? — 27 replies — 30.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
WHAT IS HAPPENING?

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-05-18 08:07 UTC

OP

WHAT IS HAPPENING?

Early replies (first 3)

62243077
Nobody wants your 10 year old internet monopoly money. The only reason it holds any value above zero is because some bots want to scam other bots on the way down.

62243086
>>62243077
So once ai agents get more independent it'll fly to the moon. Thanks for the advice.

62243088
>>62243060
No new narratives. As it stands crypto is a failed experiment with no use cases

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62243093
The crypto president betrayed us.

62243094
>>62243060
People on the fence of a 4-year cycle bear market and super cycle sold. I wouldn't worry about it though.

62243114
4 pinks wojak threads is what's happening. Crypto just bottomed after shaking out some bulls.

62243120
>>62243086
The opposite, the better trader bots become the more extreme leverage they use on minor price swings. That's why nothing moons like it used to, a holder needs a x100, a bot needs x100 leverage on a 1%+.

62243143
IT JUST TAKES SOME TIME

62243157
my asshole is getting torn apart, financially speaking

62243159
I miss the Wojak Index

62243250
>>62243077
Stocks too going

62243256
>>62243086
It's real o_O

62243279
I'm so extremely tempted to catch this falling knife but I have such an abysmal track record of timing, I don't have the courage to do it.
> thinks I bought the bottom
> crashes another 25-50%
> panic sell in pure despair capitulation after I told myself "HODL!!!!!"
> IMMEDIATELY shoots back up
> "nah just another bounce, it'll go back down"
> IT SHOOTS UP TO THE FUCKING MOON, LEAVING ME IN THE DUST
Every. time. I'm tired bros.

62243298
>>62243060
Everyone knows Us10y will be 5% before end of June. It's apocalyptic

62243305
Trump using AI to post bullshit as usual.

62243332
>>62243298
Does this mean cash is king?

62243366
>>62243332
Inflation will kill cash long term

62243373
>>62243060
Cz dumping on wm again.... I made a thread before the dump.

62243394
>>62243332
Only if you select the perfect equity in a sea of red even then it may take 5-10 years to break even in real terms relative to inflation. Gold is the only real alternative but you and it need to make it through to the otherside in tact. You'll have to make it through a pvpve server where the police response time is three days.

62243400
>>62243394
Gold performs poorly during inflationary periods

62243431
>>62243400
Depends on the period studied. In wiermar there wasn't anything better to have, even real estate was often sold at a loss. Least bad is probably ideal description. This is to say everything may perform poorly. I expect gold and silver to sell off further along with everything else until yields stabilize. The question is what happens when thats not possible? Revaluation of gold is one of only a few tricks the goverment to pull to extend its life. Keep it all in cash, bonds, crypto or equities if that suits you, you'll be amongst millions of others.

62243433
>>62243390
Yeah its cz.

62243730
We need the electricity for data centers bro, bitcoin is over.

62244002
>>62243256
Better be ready. Your asshole is getting probed.

62244054
>>62243060
You all voted for it.

62244147
NATURAL SELECTION

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 97 replies — 28.2/hr

Open thread • Replies: 97 • Updated: 2026-05-18 05:34 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62233500
>>62231072
is that legit?

62233508
>>62231066
You hate woman because you like man. Gayish bitcoiner

62233709
>>62233500
Yeah anon. There’s already almost 51k BTC staked in the protocol too, which is like $4B+ worth of BTC already participating around the ecosystem.

62233716
>>62223021
You can always keep slowly adding over time anon. Most people don’t even have close to 1 BTC.

62233736
>>62224655
it will be your opportunity as well.

62233806
>>62229570
I'm from Spain and I live well. Got small biz + doing crypto + forex + stocks. Life is good.

62233821
>>62233709
never though there's protocol exists like this

62233827
>buy btc
buy link
buy ICP
buy avax

62233835
>>62228833
And Ethereum is the godess

62233840
>>62228631
Hes just a homosexual and fears for the virginity of his anus

62233880
>>62233827

worst trash available
>UNGMI

62233986
>>62233821
Yeah anon, it already exists but I think a lot of people just still aren’t paying attention to the newer BTCFi infra yet. And it’s still pretty early too since the TBVs haven’t even launched yet, which should unlock even more native BTC use cases.

62233993
>>62225388
See this anon?

62233997
>>62224679
The funniest part is you probably think debasing fiat printed out of thin air is somehow more “real” than a globally traded asset with trillions in liquidity.

62234000
>>62230021
Gonna be dead broke without BTC lol.

62234021
>>62233806
Aren't you fucked with taxes and DAC8 now, I cannot buy anymore via a cex here

62234099
>>62233840
It sucks being gay

62234109
>>62234021
do the alternative. Privacy

62234248
>>62234021
Government is shit.

62234899
>>62233986
I don’t think that’s enough when ETH literally lives and dies by DeFi activity.

62235825
>>62222594
you don't need to

62237090
>>62220146
give me money

62237423
>>62219995
you're rich mate.

62238430
>>62229077
Exactly, but turning BTC into an active asset will through native staking, instead of just worrying about future risks.

62238954
>>62234248
I'm not surprised lol

62239105
>just buy into the obvious ponzi scheme bro

62240786
>>62228616
hmm find better opportunities

62241339
>>62219942
Soon I will get to that 1 BTC mark, RN I'm buying more and staking some.

62244007
>>62238430
That's the right move anon

62244072

item.title: KEK MICROSOFT — 20 replies — 26.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
KEK MICROSOFT

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-05-18 08:16 UTC

OP

KEK MICROSOFT

Early replies (first 3)

62242596
>>62242594
Fake news. Doesn't he own Microsoft? As far as I know that means you can't sell the stock since it's literally your company.

62242597
Just in time before a windows defender exploit

62242598
>>62242594
Micropenis lel, didnt read btw.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62242604
>>62242594
>3bn

who cares? it has like a 3T market cap, 3bn is literally nothing and doesnt matter

62242615
>>62242596
>Doesn't he own Microsoft?
No

62242641
>>62242594
Good riddance.
Ever since bill gaytes started stock dumping, it has been pumping.
Steve Ballmer held all his stock and he's now far richer than Billy boy.

62242765
>>62242596
It's a public company you tard

62242784
>>62242596
Nope. It's real.

https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/bill-gates-just-did-unthinkable-132955202.html

>The Bill Gates Foundation Trust exited its final position by selling all remaining shares after reducing holdings from 28.5M to zero shares, a move driven by portfolio diversification, valuation optimization, and funding obligations rather than confidence loss.

>Gates’ complete exit from Microsoft reflects rational asset allocation for a charitable foundation managing liquidity needs and concentration risk, not a loss of faith in Microsoft’s AI-driven business transformation and ability to convert enterprise technology investments into recurring revenue.

Methinks they doth protest too much.

62242804
1. Why did he do that?
2. What does it mean for the company's future and its stocks?

62242984
>jeet CEO
the new IBM
zero

62242991
>>62242804
I heard co-pilot licenses are about to go up like 15x the price. Maybe the AI bubble is about to pop.

62243539
>>62242594
UH MICROJEET BROS??

62243590
>>62242596
Holy shit how can people so dumb come and post on a finance board. Truly shows why this place sucks.

62243606
>>62243590
>newfag can’t decipher obvious b8 and has a Redditor meltdown

62243628
>>62243606
No it's not bait, this board is pretty low. I have 16 filtered threads right now, people are really low IQ here. Shame.

62243736
>>62243590
this board was better in 2018 when alt seasoned happened

62243990
>>62242604
Market cap doesn’t = liquidity. All that matters is liquidity. It’s why some shit coin can be worth 100 million but if liquidity is low some dude could sell 1 million dollars and price goes down 30% reducing market cap by 30 million with a 1 million sell order

62244084
>>62243628
16kek, i have 34, prolly only lankshit.

62244361
>>62242991
bullshit

the prices of m365 copilot licences are decreasing since 2023

t. copilot security engineer

62244369
>>62242596

You missed out on ZCASH, didn't you?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-05-18 08:06 UTC

OP

You missed out on ZCASH, didn't you?

Early replies (first 3)

62242844
>>62242813
Unironically bought the bottom and sold the top on this last pump. This coin glows so bright and is so Jewish I dislike putting much of my money towards it.

62242851
>>62242813
buy an ad.

62242860
Why has this coin outperformed my Chainlink stack? What does it do that is so worthy of a x10 in value?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62243066
>>62242813
scam dead shitcoin no one cares about nigga

62243259
>>62242813

I wouldn't say I missed out, but I definitely didn't buy that trash.

You'd have to be a financial illiterate to put money into a privacy coin and call it an investment.

62243265
>>62243259
You can commit tax fraud with Monero, boomer

62243388
>>62242813
What's the make it? I have 63

62243442
>>62242813
I could give you a list of things I have missed in the last few months. With every single one giving me a 10x. I don’t think about it cause playing the shoulda coulda woulda game rarely is a learning moment, it just leads to fantasizing/self-pitying and isnt helpful.

62243446
>>62243265

It didn't say that it didn't have uses, I said that it is not a serious investment.

62243500
>>62242813
Last time zogchow made a move like this XMR mogged it to $800, it was off a hack but still. Even the main ecelebs shilling this kikecoin acknowledges that XMR mogs it to oblivion on every technical and usecase and that they're only shilling Zcash because it has CEX liquidity. So you're essentially buying a privacy memecoin that has the entire floor bought up by e-celebs from a year ago in a market that is rotating the same liquidity more and more violently

62243706
>>62243500
>Last time zogchow made a move like this XMR mogged it to $800
can't even imagine the seethe when XMR will be $10k

62244080
>>62243706

It won't be, without hyperinflation.

62244215
>>62242813

62244241
>>62244080

62244360

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 75 replies — 15.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-05-18 04:13 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62238015
>>62237934
Anon, I'd like some constructive feedback. Regardless of whether the approach is simple or not, the fact of the matter is that the data backs it up. Only one of the lines uses ORB.

The full blend, as shown in the first picture, is split across 5 different lines. The correlation between them, while not perfectly non-overlapping, is still low enough that it’s rare for them all to lose on the same day.

In the days since I made this, I was actually able to find a way to further cut down a few outliers, reducing total trades taken by 16% while increasing points by about 9%.

But these are just marginal improvements, and I’m hesitant to keep chasing them due to fears of overfitting.

My hypothesis is that, with how many trades I take, I should be able to quantitatively chop down the worst of them. I’ve already managed to do that with relatively simple filters over the past few days, but I’m running up against a wall, so I want to avoid going too far. THAT's why I made this thread. In hopes of finding someone with knowledge.

62238067
>>62238015
ok, now we're getting somewhere. you are afraid of overfitting and not even using ML lmfao noob

62238083
>>62238067
Rather than implement something I have no understanding of, I'm sticking to what I do know. I'll say it again. if you wanna offer criticism or advice, keep it constructive. just saying "ML" is so vague. Be more specific. ML in what way?

62238370
>>62238083
any sort of model.predict() is going to overfit unless you train on like 20-30% of the data thats implicitly going to be trained or predicted on, so with pure ta or price action strategies overfitting is never possible since theyre raw calculations of mathematics on price action time series data


holy shit anon you should know this im sharing liquid gold intuition with you

62238387
>>62219625
That equity curve is either a data issue or clearly just picking pennies up infront of a steamroller.
You being obssessed with "smoothness" is also a red flag.
You thinking "the data backs it up" with confidence is also a BIG red flag, this is not how real trading works.
Anyone can just run some lookahead biased regressions and uncover a "strategy", anything that is actually statistically robust and ACTIONABLE will be based off of forward walks and have effect sizes so small you'll be questioning whether its even real, and only THEN have you found something that might actually indeed be real.

62238401
>>62238370
I tried a variety of strategies based on price action but I could never get any of them to work. This setup is the best I could get. I tried other things but they wouldn’t work. I’m still not understanding how this ML would filter out losses in a way that’s actionable. If it just gives me some highly specific bucket like “trades between 0.75% and 0.84% of OR width are losing” then that’s just over fitting.

>>62238387
I’ve mentioned why. It’s for the sake of prop firms. They have hard drawdown rules. As mentioned before, i can theoretically pass and even get on a funded account with the strat as it is now. But it takes too long to pass and too long to get a payout. Most of what I read in ur response sounds like fluff no offense. I’m all for constructive criticism but I don’t see how any of what you said applies to my.

My strategy is a blend of 5 simple ideas that each trade on their own with the idea of:

Rather than find one single strategy that is good and trades often,
Find multiple good strategies that don’t trade often individually and then use them together.

I haven’t done any look back biased regressions, I have a separate 2026 only sample that I’ll test my strategies on to make sure they’re not overfit. And they’ll work just fine on new never before seen data.

Guys I am open to any constructive criticism, just please make it make sense in the context of what I’m asking.

62238500
>>62238401
Listen bro you don't just throw a couple of funny numbers in a computer and cash comes out thats just not how the world works.
You believe you need to be super opaque so we don't "steal" your strategy, but there are thousands of guys like you who think they have some top secret "strat" that they just need someone to fund them for.
What model are you even using? Some GARCH? HMM? Frequency of your data?

62238660
> top secret strategy trades the Nasdaq, an index that only goes up
> it makes money
> anon thinks he's a genius
> loses to buy-and-hold TQQQ

62238773
>>62238500
I’m not being opaque. I said I want advice from someone who has knowledge in fields related to statistics and quantitative analysis. Do you?

No, I don’t use GARCH. My strategy does not tune itself, it does not use some regressive or adjusting method. It follows the same rules trading in 2021 as it does in 2025 in the data set. The TP and SL’s are the only dynamic variable because they are based on VWAP, or ORB, or the volume profile as well as fixed % based on the open market value. A 20 point move in 2020 is far different than a 20 point move in 2025.
But they are not hand tuned or adjusted by me. It sets the TP based on the given range. It’s as simple as that.

I only used volume and OHLC of NQ and ES via 1m candles.

Also who the hell said I need funding? You should read all of my messages before making assumptions.

>>62238660
The worst drawdowns were approximately 3,000 points, while peak equity was around 50,000 points.

This is just napkin math, but let’s make a few simplifying assumptions:

Assume 1 NQ contract per trade. Some lines had multiple take-profits, but we’ll simplify. NQ is $20 per point, so a 3,000-point drawdown would require $60,000 in capital to withstand the worst observed drawdown. To be more conservative, let’s round that up to $100,000 of starting cash. Unexessary but let’s do it anyways.

Starting with $100,000, if running equity reaches 50,000 points, that’s 50,000 × $20 = $1,000,000.

So, theoretically, starting with $100,000 and ending around $1 million is possible under these assumptions.

TQQQ went up about 230% from 2020 till 2025, where my data ends.

230% vs 900%.

If I had the money, I’d just put it all into SCHD and chill. But I don’t. That’s why I made this thread. That’s why I’m making this strategy and asking if anyone here can offer ideas that make sense in the context of what I’m asking.

62238820
>>62238773
duh you don't hold TQQQ through 2022 retard
I already understand you don't want to apply any brains but jesus

62239204
>>62238500
lmfao, garch, mm, and HMM are all useless in trading

62239213
>>62238773
try this just for the lols and its like throwing shit to a wall hoping it sticks

code the strategy using an external data api use every talib indicator an oscilator then denoise the indicators then shove that as a filter and vectorize the backtest extract the highest sharpe ratio or net return in similar sharpe give or take in your datamining script

62239220
>>62239213
if you dont know anything about genetic algorithms watch coding trains NEAT flappy bird series its quite useful information and intuition for genetic algorithms doesnt have to be neuroevolution since these are just indicators you wont be topologically ranking these to another sorting neuron layer you just need to mutate and generate fitness effectively

62239225
if all of this is above your head just fucking quit quant isnt cut out for noobs its cut out for mathematics genius' and physicists

62239227
also i gave your ass free alpha i expect you to post source code now

62240221
>>62239213
I already tried something similar to this. I didn’t cover the entire library, but I used a more pointed approach that was computationally feasible for me and something I could actually verify myself.

The issue is that a lot of strategies perform all right, but they’re not smooth enough for a prop firm. What I’m showing is not the only thing I found, but it’s the smoothest thing I’ve found so far.

The others either weren’t as smooth or had large drawdown spikes.

Also there's no such thing as a single indicator that will perform well. If I do find sometrhing, it's going to be a combination of some, so I'd have to test a lot of permutations. Walking forward through millions of candles, testing hundreds if not thousands of combos, and then multiplying that again to find the optimal TP / SL setup. that is going to take a lot of computation and a hell of a lot of time of waiting. Not a smart approach.

Besides, I don't need a new strategy. I already have one. I just need to optimize it without suffering from overfitting. The only way I could do that is to have some new static filter. One thing I was looking at was introducing volumetrics and using that to filter bad trades, and it actually did help.

What I might do is try to combine the library with what I currently have and see if it improves in some way. Though that does open a whole can of worms. We'll see.


>>62239220
Flappy Bird is a clean fixed environment. trading ain't.

62240722
>>62240221
Nvm you can’t be helped you’re just trying to act smart at this point lmfao

62240724
bump once more before I wake up

62240823
>>62240221
do you even know why i recommended you to use a genetic algorithm? or you just blab shit like flappy bird if a fixed env? its about the intuition for GA's lol you are stupid as fuck nigga

62240835
>>62224277
Have you tried filtering on volatility anon? It tends to help trend following system. It could be something as simple as an inside bar chart pattern.

62240836
>>62240835
Don’t help him he’s retarded

62240887
>>62240835
I actually did try that. I tried filtering over a few fixed periods. However, all the trades that would be cut out would also include winners in an equal ratio. I couldn't really get any sort of volatility filter to cut out only losers.

I've been a bit hesitant to implement a filter that's based off of candlestick patterns, just because I think that's the weakest form of analysis. Analysis based on volumetrics or analysis based on trends or volatility, etc., that makes more sense to me compared to just analysis based on “price is forming an inverted cup or an upside-down H”

That's been my results from a lot of filters. All the filters will cut out trades, but they'll cut out an equal amount of wins and losses. Or if they do cut out more losses, the ratio is not really that much. I am still sure that there is some method that I could use, because I'm averaging eight trades a day. In my mind, surely there has to be a way to cut that down to just two and drastically increase win rate.

62241679
i understand about 2% of what you guys are bickering about, but here's a ChatGPT QRD of the thread cause it's funny

62242217
>>62241679
lol then dont comment im a pro

62242682
>>62241679
I've been asking the same question but people don't seem to understand it.
I already have a working strategy. I want to improve it. I've explained the avenue I want to go down to improve it, and I've asked if that's even possible.

90% of the responses have been irrelevant, like telling me to go in a vague ML direction or telling me to start from scratch looking at new strategies.

62242797
>>62242682
orb is shit nigga lmao

62243824
>>62242682
win rate is not as important as the type of risk to return you have. you want to make sure you can find and take as many trades as possible that fits your specific strategy. you want to win ideally more then 3x your risk so no matter what you dont go to zero. the longer you play the game without going bust the better your odds are of becoming the 1% that become a profitable trader long term. and i dont mean purely expected value tables you need to understand your own set up to know if a stop limit if best or if youre pricing things to zero etc.

62243848
>>62243824
Winrate, Profit Factor, R2 ratio, and ideally 1-2 trades a day. That's what I measure my strategies by. But PF and R2 are the more important ones.

I tried to implement a 3:1 and 2:1RR strategy but it's performance metrics were not good enough, specifically PF.

>the better your odds are of becoming the 1% that become a profitable trader long term
the strategy IS profitable long term. It's just not suited for prop firm rules.

62243869
>>62243848
regardless of how you slice it your returns need to be larger then. i would look back at my losing trades that had the same setup and try to find another way to look what different about it vs the ones that were successful, also try to do the same thing on inverse products or something with an obvious downtrend and see if you can find out a new way to rule out the less likely versions of your setup. for instance youd likely get less opporutnities in a bear trend and can see why that specific time worked there something like that might benefit you

62243906
>>62243869
>see if you can find out a new way to rule out the less likely versions of your setup
That's what im looking for ATM, i don't need more trades I need less trades of higher quality. But doing that is easier said than done lol.

I am currently looking into some higher time frame analysis, trying to see if I can change how the strategy works based on regime, and maybe some time specific stuff; trades going into lunch time are less likely to win etc. But I don't think these will take my strategy from 54% to 65% plus, i need something more but im not sure.
